insights: Semrush only sees one ranking term — "scarpers" — and even that's at #6. The brand is too new for Semrush's UK crawler to have indexed much. Real Google data (Search Console tab) will show more.

All 'organic competitors' are unrelated (dictionaries, a tour company, a shoe brand) — they only overlap because they happen to mention the word 'scarpers'. Ignore until we rank for actual running terms.

Three target keywords are 0/100 difficulty: 'ai running coach', 'personalised running plan', 'best running app uk'. These are genuine open lanes — small volume but easy to win and high commercial intent.
'10k training plan' (3,600/mo, KDI 27) is the standout opportunity: real demand, easy difficulty, and we already have a dedicated landing page.

Zero backlinks detected. This is the single biggest blocker — every link from a running blog, parkrun forum, or Reddit thread compounds. Pursuing 5–10 quality backlinks would unlock most of the easy-difficulty targets.
actions: Get the /10k-training-plan page indexed and start earning backlinks to it — 3,600/mo searches and KDI 27 is the highest-ROI target.
Write 1 blog post per fortnight targeting one easy keyword each: 'AI running coach', 'personalised running plan', 'best running app UK', 'free running plan'.
Submit Scarpers to running-app roundup posts and Reddit threads (r/running, r/AdvancedRunning, r/UKrunning) — this is how zero-backlink sites bootstrap authority.
Use the live Search Console tab as the source of truth for ranking changes — Semrush is weeks behind for new sites.
Re-check Semrush in 4–6 weeks once Google has crawled more of the site; expect target keywords to start appearing in topKeywords.
